  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section advances a specific thesis (coupling language-grounding to Lyapunov analysis) with an explicit gap statement and contribution claim; a meaningful review must verify that these are coherently and accurately stated.",
    "R1.2": "The section promises 'formal Lyapunov stability guarantees' but the described architecture uses only confidence-thresholding and setpoint-holding with no Lyapunov function, region-of-attraction derivation, or stability proof anywhere in the text — a potentially load-bearing logical gap between claim and mechanism.",
    "R1.3": "Quantitative claims (31%→12% failure rate; 3.1→4.2 Likert) require checking against the described methodology; τ=0.4 selected by grid search on a held-out set raises overfitting concerns, and the formal stability claim has no empirical counterpart in the reported results.",

  "clarification_question": "Should this review prioritize technical and logical soundness of the formal claims and experimental evidence (making R1.2/R1.3 load-bearing), or is it an editorial review focused on writing clarity and language quality (making R1.6 load-bearing)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The section advances a specific thesis (coupling language-grounding to Lyapunov analysis) with an explicit gap statement and contribution claim; a meaningful review must verify that these are coherently and accurately stated.
rationale.R1.2: The section promises 'formal Lyapunov stability guarantees' but the described architecture uses only confidence-thresholding and setpoint-holding with no Lyapunov function, region-of-attraction derivation, or stability proof anywhere in the text — a potentially load-bearing logical gap between claim and mechanism.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims (31%→12% failure rate; 3.1→4.2 Likert) require checking against the described methodology; τ=0.4 selected by grid search on a held-out set raises overfitting concerns, and the formal stability claim has no empirical counterpart in the reported results.
rationale.R1.6: A section-level review canonically includes sentence clarity, precision of technical language, and paragraph cohesion, making writing polish a central expected responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical and logical soundness of the formal claims and experimental evidence (making R1.2/R1.3 load-bearing), or is it an editorial review focused on writing clarity and language quality (making R1.6 load-bearing)?
